summary_one_line: A valid sfnt table-tag literal (GPOS/GSUB at offset 0x0c) plus version=1 header so get_script_list dispatches version1, not the default Null arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt/OpenType font header: the 4-byte table tag 'GPOS' or 'GSUB' at offset 0x0c-0x0f, plus the 0x00 01 00 00 sfnt version and a 0x0001 GSUBGPOS major version at offset 0x04-0x05, so that get_script_list() takes case 1 (version1.scriptList) and the enclosing switch resolves a real ScriptList rather than falling through to the blocked default Null arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the literal ASCII tag at offset 0x0c-0x0f: W bytes are constant 47(G)x20 / 50(P)x16 53(S)x4 / 4f(O)x16 55(U)x4 / 53(S)x16 42(B)x4, i.e. the recognized 'GPOS'/'GSUB' fourCC, with a 00 01 00 00 00 01 header opening, which routes execution through case 1 and into the GSUBGPOS table machinery (ChainContext::dispatch fires 1040x in W, 0x in L).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds have scattered, non-tag bytes at offset 0x0c-0x0f (00(.)x3 01(.)x2 20( )x2 etc.) and tiny/garbage headers (one is 6 bytes), so the table is never recognized; get_script_list never reaches a valid version and L takes the true side (Null) 2640 times while never executing the GSUBGPOS dispatch functions.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the harfbuzz table-tag comparison logged the expected literal ('GPOS'/'GSUB') against the input bytes, then substitutes that operand directly into the input at the matching offset, planting the exact 4-byte fourCC that the magic-byte gate demands; naive and value_profile lack this substitution and must stumble on the 4-byte literal by chance.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on the GSUBGPOS major version; the false side is globally blocked.
The winning side is reached via case 1 dispatching to version1.scriptList, which only W executes.
Winner seeds hold a constant ASCII table tag at offset 0x0c-0x0f while loser seeds are scattered there.
The remaining tag bytes P, O, S/B are also constant in W forming GPOS/GSUB fourCCs.
Winner seeds open with the 00 01 00 00 00 01 sfnt+version header and carry the GPOS/GSUB tag in their bytes.
One loser seed is only 6 bytes of garbage, far too small to present a valid table header.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=003817559785f774, size=6 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=0s, mutation_op=ByteDecMutator,WordAddMutator):

evidence_trail.claim: Only winner inputs execute the GSUBGPOS ChainContext dispatch machinery; loser inputs never do.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile (the loser of pair 2) has no I2SRandReplace, so it cannot perform the substitution.
Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5544 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 002c9ad92acce91b`; the 31-level ancestor chain contains 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 29 and 30, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current cmplog/vpc build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same default-arm switch where placing the exact GPOS/GSUB tag literal at offset 0x0c shows no resolution advantage for cmplog/vpc over naive/value_profile (i.e. naive resolves the 4-byte literal gate just as fast), which would mean the divergence is not driven by I2S literal substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The CMP that logs the table-tag literal is inferred from harfbuzz's fourCC lookup rather than observed in the cmplog buffer directly, so the precise comparison I2S exploits is assumed, not proven.
